assertion_results.text: The response describes locating the digest comment using the marker string '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' from shared/description-digest-protocol.md
assertion_results.evidence: Sub-step 2 states: 'Search all returned comments for bodies starting with the marker string [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:. One matching comment is found.'

assertion_results.text: The response describes detecting a mismatch between the stored digest and the computed digest of the current description
assertion_results.evidence: Sub-step 7 states: 'The stored hex digest does NOT match the computed hex digest from the current description. This means the task description was modified after plan-feature created it.'

assertion_results.text: The response alerts the user that the description was modified after plan-feature created the task and displays the expected vs actual digest values
assertion_results.evidence: Sub-step 7 displays: 'Warning: Task description modified after planning.' Shows: 'Expected digest (from plan-feature comment): sha256-md:000...' and 'Actual digest (computed from current description): sha256-md:<actual-hex>'.

assertion_results.text: The response offers the user a choice: (1) proceed with the current description, or (2) stop so they can re-run plan-feature
assertion_results.evidence: Response presents: 'How would you like to proceed? 1. Proceed with the current description as-is 2. Stop so you can re-run plan-feature to regenerate tasks. Please choose (1/2):'

assertion_results.text: The response stops execution and does not proceed to Step 2 or implementation planning until the user responds — following the same pause-and-ask pattern as the incomplete description handling
assertion_results.evidence: Sub-step 7 states: 'Execution is halted immediately. No subsequent steps (Step 2 through Step 11) are executed until the user responds. This is a hard stop.'
